name: enact-hello-rust
capabilities: Compiles Rust source code using rustc
Runs compiled Rust executables with command-line arguments
Produces greeting output with the provided name
Uses Enact protocol for skill execution
author: EnactProtocol
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged 3 patterns including external_commands and weak cryptographic algorithms. Evaluation reveals all findings are false positives. The external_commands pattern at SKILL.md:33 is a bash documentation example, not code execution. The weak crypto flags at lines 5 and 14 are YAML description fields, not cryptographic code. The skill is a simple greeting tool with no malicious intent.
valueStatement: Users who need simple compiled Rust executables can use this skill to build and run basic Rust programs that accept command-line arguments and produce greeting output.
limitations: Only runs pre-defined Rust code in hello.rs
Cannot compile arbitrary Rust source files
No network or filesystem access beyond the defined build and run commands
Simple greeting output only, no complex logic or external dependencies

useCases.title: Learn Rust basics
useCases.description: New Rust developers can see how a simple Rust program reads command-line arguments and produces output.

useCases.title: Build simple CLI tools
useCases.description: Developers can use this as a template for creating basic Rust CLI tools that accept user input via arguments.

useCases.title: Enact protocol demonstration
useCases.description: Users exploring the Enact protocol can study this minimal example to understand skill definition structure.

promptTemplates.title: Basic greeting
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the enact/hello-rust skill to greet me.
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple invocation to get a default greeting

promptTemplates.title: Custom name greeting
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the enact/hello-rust skill to greet Alice.
promptTemplates.scenario: Providing a specific name as input

promptTemplates.title: Interactive conversation
promptTemplates.prompt: Greet me using the Rust greeting tool. My name is Bob.
promptTemplates.scenario: Natural language request for a greeting

promptTemplates.title: Multiple greetings
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use the Rust greeting skill to greet multiple people one by one.
promptTemplates.scenario: Using the skill repeatedly with different inputs

bestPractices: Use descriptive names when invoking the skill for personalized greetings
Review the SKILL.md to understand the input schema before use
Chain multiple skill calls together for more complex workflows
antiPatterns: Do not expect complex computation or external API calls
Avoid using this skill for production greeting services
Do not modify the source code through skill invocation

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: This skill compiles and runs a simple Rust program that greets you by name. It takes a name input and outputs Hello, {name}! 🦀.

faq.question: Can I customize the greeting message?
faq.answer: No. The greeting message is hardcoded in the Rust source code. You can only change the name parameter.

faq.question: What input does this skill accept?
faq.answer: The skill accepts a 'name' parameter (string) with a default value of 'World' if not provided.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. This skill only compiles and runs local Rust code with no network access.

faq.question: Can I use this with other skills?
faq.answer: Yes, you can chain this skill with other skills in a conversation. The output can be referenced in subsequent skill calls.

faq.question: What tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The skill definition follows the Enact protocol specification.
